addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

91	typedef struct ZopfliHash
92	{
93	    int *head;            /* Hash value to index of its most recent occurrence. */
94	    unsigned short *prev; /* Index to index of prev. occurrence of same hash. */
95	    int *hashval;         /* Index to hash value at this index. */
96	    int val;              /* Current hash value. */
97	
98	    /* Fields with similar purpose as the above hash, but for the second hash with
99	    a value that is calculated differently.  */
100	    int *head2;            /* Hash value to index of its most recent occurrence. */
101	    unsigned short *prev2; /* Index to index of prev. occurrence of same hash. */
102	    int *hashval2;         /* Index to hash value at this index. */
103	    int val2;              /* Current hash value. */
104	
105	    unsigned short *same; /* Amount of repetitions of same byte after this .*/
106	} ZopfliHash;
107	
108	typedef struct Node Node;
109	
110	/*
111	Nodes forming chains. Also used to represent leaves.
112	*/
113	struct Node
114	{
115	    size_t weight; /* Total weight (symbol count) of this chain. */
116	    Node *tail;    /* Previous node(s) of this chain, or 0 if none. */
117	    int count;     /* Leaf symbol index, or number of leaves before this chain. */
118	};
119	
120	/*
121	Memory pool for nodes.
122	*/
123	typedef struct NodePool
124	{
125	    Node *next; /* Pointer to a free node in the pool. */
126	} NodePool;
127	
128	/*
129	Cache used by ZopfliFindLongestMatch to remember previously found length/dist
130	values.
131	This is needed because the squeeze runs will ask these values multiple times for
132	the same position.
133	Uses large amounts of memory, since it has to remember the distance belonging
134	to every possible shorter-than-the-best length (the so called "sublen" array).
135	*/
136	typedef struct ZopfliLongestMatchCache
137	{
138	    unsigned short *length;
139	    unsigned short *dist;
140	    unsigned char *sublen;
141	} ZopfliLongestMatchCache;

148	{
149	    /* Whether to print output */
150	    int verbose;
151	
152	    /* Whether to print more detailed output */
153	    int verbose_more;
154	
155	    /*
156	    Maximum amount of times to rerun forward and backward pass to optimize LZ77
157	    compression cost. Good values: 10, 15 for small files, 5 for files over
158	    several MB in size or it will be too slow.
159	    */
160	    int numiterations;
161	
162	    /*
163	    If true, splits the data in multiple deflate blocks with optimal choice
164	    for the block boundaries. Block splitting gives better compression. Default:
165	    true (1).
166	    */
167	    int blocksplitting;
168	
169	    /*
170	    No longer used, left for compatibility.
171	    */
172	    int blocksplittinglast;
173	
174	    /*
175	    Maximum amount of blocks to split into (0 for unlimited, but this can give

177	    */
178	    int blocksplittingmax;
179	} ZopfliOptions;
180	
181	/*
182	Some state information for compressing a block.
183	This is currently a bit under-used (with mainly only the longest match cache),
184	but is kept for easy future expansion.
185	*/
186	typedef struct ZopfliBlockState

194	    size_t blockstart;

content.content: The file /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: size_t pos, size_t size, size_t limit,
unsigned short *sublen, unsigned short *distance, unsigned short *length)
+/* Block state and longest-match cache, as required by the callees
+/* The cache slot for this position is unfilled (length==1, dist==0), which is the
+   precondition StoreInLongestMatchCache needs in order to store a fresh value. */
+/* The hash structure: each of the two hashes has a head/prev/hashval array plus
+/* Hash-chain well-formedness needed for memory safety of the chain walk:
+   the head entry for the current hash value points at this position, and every
+   prev link stays inside the sliding window. */
+/* The chain only visits indices whose stored hash value matches the current
+   hash value (an invariant maintained while the hash is built). */
+/* Output parameters. sublen must be a 259-element array (callers that pass null
+   are out of scope of this contract, since the cache callees require it fresh). */
unsigned short hpos = pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK, p, pp;

content.text:
Confirmed: **0/157 killed, verification passes (exit 0) but vacuously** — exactly the documented multi-pointer `is_fresh` depth-200 pattern. This function has ~17 fresh objects (`s`, `lmc`, `length`, `dist`, `sublen`(lmc), `h`, `head`, `prev`, `hashval`, `head2`, `prev2`, `hashval2`, `same`, `array`, `distance`, `length`, `sublen`(param)), far exceeding CBMC's depth bound, so the precondition conjunction is UNSAT and every mutant survives unobserved. I cannot reduce the pointer count (can't change the C code), and loop contracts are silently ignored here, so the kill score cannot realistically be improved.

Let me record this finding.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity
description: ZopfliFindLongestMatch verifies vacuously 0/157 — ~17 is_fresh objects exceed CBMC depth bound
metadata:
  type: project
---

`ZopfliFindLongestMatch` in zopfli.c verifies (exit 0) but **vacuously, 0/157 kills**
(156 survived, 1 compile-fail). Same root cause as [[cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity]] and
[[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]: the sound spec needs ~17 `__CPROVER_is_fresh`
objects (s, s->lmc + its length/dist/sublen arrays, h + its head/prev/hashval/
head2/prev2/hashval2/same arrays, array, distance, length, sublen), whose malloc
harness exceeds the depth bound → precondition conjunction UNSAT → all mutants survive.

The spec is strong & sound: hash-chain well-formedness (head[val]==hpos, forall
prev[i]<WINDOW_SIZE, forall hashval[i]==val for both hashes), cache slot ==1/dist==0
to satisfy StoreInLongestMatchCache, array fresh of `size`, limit in [3,258],
ensures `*length<=258 && pos+*length<=size`. Left in place.

Kill score cannot be raised: can't reduce pointer-param count (no C edits allowed),
loop contracts silently ignored ([[cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored]]). Gotcha: each
`__CPROVER_forall` block needs a UNIQUE quantifier var name across the whole
contract (reused `i` → "redeclaration with no linkage" CONVERSION ERROR).

content.content: <system-reminder>Warning: the file exists but is shorter than the provided offset (12). The file has 12 lines.</system-reminder>

1	- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
2	- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
3	- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
4	- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
5	- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
6	- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
7	- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
8	- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
9	- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
10	- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
11	- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
12

- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)

- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills

- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place

- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)

content.text:
Done. Summary:

## ZopfliFindLongestMatch — verified (vacuously)

I added a strong, sound CBMC contract to `ZopfliFindLongestMatch` (zopfli.c:2569). CBMC reports **Verified** (exit 0), but mutation testing yields **0/157 kills** — a vacuous pass.

**The spec captures:**
- All `is_fresh` allocations needed by the cache callees (`s`, `s->lmc` + its `length`/`dist`/`sublen` arrays) and the hash structure (`h` + `head`/`prev`/`hashval`/`head2`/`prev2`/`hashval2`/`same`).
- Cache slot precondition (`length[lmcpos]==1`, `dist==0`) so `StoreInLongestMatchCache`'s contract is satisfiable.
- Hash-chain well-formedness for memory safety: `head[val] == pos & WINDOW_MASK`, `∀ prev[i] < WINDOW_SIZE` (both hashes), and `∀ hashval[i] == val` so the in-body chain-walk assertions hold.
- Input window (`array` fresh of `size`, `pos < size`, `limit ∈ [3,258]`) and outputs (`distance`/`length`/259-element `sublen` fresh).
- `assigns` for the two output shorts, the param `sublen`, and the cache slot/`sublen`; `ensures(*length <= 258 && pos + *length <= size)`.

**Why 0 kills (and why it can't be improved):** the sound spec requires ~17 distinct `is_fresh` objects. Their malloc harness exceeds CBMC's depth bound, making the precondition conjunction unsatisfiable, so every mutant survives unobserved. This is the same documented pattern as `TryGetFromLongestMatchCache` and the general depth-200 `is_fresh` vacuity. I can't reduce the pointer-parameter count (no C edits allowed) and loop contracts are silently ignored here, so the kill score cannot realistically rise. The strong sound spec is left in place, and I recorded the finding (plus the gotcha that each `__CPROVER_forall` needs a unique quantifier variable name) in memory.
